overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved multiple ordinances including rezonings and a modified speed limit ordinance for Weston Avenue, rejected the original speed limit proposal 4-3 before passing an amended version keeping Von Kennel to Highway J at 45 mph. The board heard passionate public comment criticizing the approach to funding the fire department through referendum rather than budget prioritization, and acknowledged significant staff efforts during a recent historic blizzard.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public comments - Jim Pensel on fire department funding
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions
Department reports (Administrator, Clerk, Finance, Fire/EMS, Parks/Rec, Planning, Police, Public Works, Technology)
Ordinances - Rezonings and speed limit amendments
Resolution - Hinter Springs Second Edition subdivision final plat
New business items including e-bike ordinance, parking restrictions, park agreements, and utility contracts

discussions.body: Jim Pensel of 5002 Aerrol Street spoke critically about the village's approach to funding SAFER. He attended the SAFER Citizen Academy and witnessed understaffing firsthand. He criticized the referendum approach as 'kicking the can down the road' since the $600,000 levy has no sunset date and costs will continue to increase. He called for budget reprioritization, stating 'We have the money. You just need to have the courage to spend it where it actually matters.' Finance Director Jessica responded that the village cannot borrow for additional firefighters, only for capital projects like the Kennedy Park turf, and defended staff efforts noting they are 'the cheapest' and 'most efficient.'

The original ordinance to change speed limits including setting Weston Avenue from Von Kennel to Ryan at 35 mph failed 3-4, with Maloney, Jordan, and the presiding officer voting no. Trustee Maloney argued the road section was too sparse for 35 mph compared to other village roads. An amended motion by Maloney, seconded by Jordan, was then passed to keep Von Kennel to Highway J at 45 mph while setting Camp Phillips to Von Kennel at 35 mph, with all other ordinance changes intact.

Two rezoning ordinances were approved unanimously as recommended by planning commission: Ordinance 26-00004 rezoning portion of 8905 Bert Street from RR5 to SFS, and Ordinance 26-00005 rezoning portion of 7105 Christensen Avenue from SL to SFS single family residential small lot.

discussions.item: Intersection Signage at Community Center Drive and Birch Street
discussions.body: The board approved changing the stop sign to a yield sign with a friendly amendment adding a stop sign for bicyclists coming off the pedestrian bridge. Trustee Hooang raised safety concerns about cyclists coming off the bridge at 15-20 mph with no signal to stop. The amended motion passed unanimously.

A 10-year user agreement was approved unanimously. The committee recommended the longer term because youth organizations are investing money at Kennedy Park and shouldn't risk losing access after one year. The agreement formalizes expectations that have existed informally for over 10 years.
Trustee Lewis recommended postponing discussion until the next meeting so the newly seated board could make this decision. Motion to defer passed unanimously.
Approved unanimously. Starting next term, trustees will use Microsoft Teams instead of text messaging for inter-trustee communication. A training session will be held when the new board convenes.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Aerrol Street spoke for approximately 4 minutes criticizing the village's approach to funding the fire department. He praised SAFER staff after attending their citizen academy but criticized the referendum approach as inadequate long-term, arguing the board should reprioritize existing budget funds rather than asking taxpayers for additional levies. He specifically mentioned the Kennedy Park turf project and aquatic center as 'wants' versus fire and police as 'needs.'
actionItems: Speed limit ordinance amended: Von Kennel to Highway J remains 45 mph; Camp Phillips to Von Kennel set at 35 mph
Rezoning of portions of 8905 Bert Street and 7105 Christensen Avenue approved
Hinter Springs Second Edition subdivision final plat approved
E-bike/euro ordinance discussion tabled pending county completion of their process
No parking restrictions removed on west side of Alderson Street along Kennedy Park
Stop sign changed to yield sign at Community Center Drive/Birch Street with bicycle stop sign added
10-year baseball/softball field maintenance agreement approved
Commercial rotary mower purchase approved
Park shelter fees and field rental costs approved
Eagle Scout project at McKiller Park approved with funding from park operations
Remote meeting attendance policy deferred to next meeting for new board consideration
Microsoft Teams approved for trustee communications starting next term
Military Road utility engineering service contract approved
Business 51 storm pond engineering amendment approved for $13,500
Sewer televising software contract approved
2026 annual stream maintenance plan budget approved
Hospital area repaving change order #4 approved
Well 2 rehabilitation approved
Sign encroachment agreement with Mo Floor Coverings approved
Two more referendum informational sessions scheduled: March 31 (4:30-6pm) and April 2 (12-1:30pm)
Next meeting April 21st at 6 PM with new board members
title: Board of Trustees - 3/23/2026 - 3/23/2026
